calendar.matters:
Motion To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-21-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Alan A. Dressler, Esq., 415-421-7980, for petitioner and moving party William Noble Post
Raymond Hamilton, Esq., 510-622-2223, for respondent and opposing party Chon Gutierrez, Department of Motor Vehicles

RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM POST'S Motion To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is GRANTED. Order signed. (301/AJR/eh) Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620

Stay Of Suspension Of Petitoner'S Driver'S License, & Setting.
Off Calendar Mar-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Petitioner william Noble's request of stay of suspension of petitioner's Driver's License & Setting-previously stayed per court order Dept. 301 11/3/04. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Motion To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Mar-09-2005 Continued To Mar-21-05 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: William Noble's petition for writ of mandate-continued to 3/21/05 on the court's own motion. Respondent is directed to provide courtesy copies of their opposition papers and administrative record to Dept. 301 ASAP with a cover letter referencing the continued hearing date. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
